MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.
Off Calendar Oct-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Jay Harker, Esq., Clausen Miller PC, (949) 260-3100, for Plaintiff.

Megan B. Pham, Esq., BREMER WHYTE BROWN & O'MEARA LLP, (510) 540-4881, for Defendant Ideal Restoration.

CAITLIN B. WILEY-WALKER, ESQ., EDLIN GALLAGHER HUIE & BLUM, (415) 397-9006, for Defendant Deehan Plumbing, Inc.

RE: DEEHAN PLUMBING, INC.'s MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.

Ruling is argued. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Deehan Plumbing, Inc.'s "renewed application for determination of good faith settlement" is granted. Deehan's previous application was denied without prejudice, because the information provided was too sketchy. While still no model of clarity, the renewed application provides enough to allow a "rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Scott & Warner Builders, Inc. (SWB) was general contractor on a home reconstruction project that had an interior flooding event. SWB's insurer paid the homeowner $280,301 and points a liability finger at Ideal Restoration, Inc., which points to Deehan. Deehan seeks a good faith determination for a $35,000 settlement; Ideal opposes. Deehan approximates its proportion of the liability at 10-15%, relying largely on a declaration from SWB's job superintendent. Ideal, which (unlike Deehan) was working where the leak occurred, says the $35,000 is "convinently [sic] inconsequential and self-determined." However, as between the two subcontractors, no reason for bias by the superintendent is suggested.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with crc 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-01-2023 Continued To Mar-04-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Notice Of Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jay Harker, Esq., via Zoom, CLAUSEN MILLER P.C., (949) 260-3100, for Plaintiff.

Megan B. Pham, Esq., via Zoom, BREMER WHYTE BROWN & O'MEARA LLP, (510) 540-4881, for Defendant.

Caitlin Wiley-Walker, Esq., in person, Edlin Gallagher Huie + Blum, (415) 403-4406, for Cross-Defendant.

RE: Cross-Defendant DEEHAN PLUMBING, INC.'s Application for Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Ruling - ARGUED. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Deehan Plumbing, Inc.'s "application for determination of good faith settlement" is denied without prejudice. Scott & Warner Builders, Inc. was general contractor on a home reconstruction project that had a flooding event. There were "several subcontractors," including Deehan and Ideal Restoration, Inc. (Pham Dec. 2:5-7.) SWB's insurer apparently paid the homeowner some unstated amount of money and seeks $280,301 from some unstated set of the several subcontractors for unstated liabilities. Deehan seeks to settle for $35,000; Ideal opposes. A court's first and foremost job on one of these motions is a "rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) This the court cannot do with the sketchy information provided.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)

***After hearing, an order is signed. (See scanned order for details)
